text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy little town of Willowbrook, there lived an old wise owl named Oliver. Oliver knew everything. He knew every bird's secret, every creature's tale.

One misty morning, Oliver decided he just couldn't ignore Gerald Mouse. Gerald was always bragging about his latest invention—a small flashlight that could illuminate dark places. That sounded cool.

Oliver figured Gerald couldn't invent anything mysterious. Maybe there was a map hidden there.

That evening, Oliver snooped around town. He pecked his way under the hedge where books were tucked away.

With trembling feathers, Oliver pulled open an ancient tome. The candle flickered. "Dark caves?" he whispered.

Yes!

He checked elsewhere. He fluttered through orchards. His tiny owl ears took everything in.

Then he found it—a small bird clutching pearls.

"Why pearls?" Oliver heard.

"They treasure secrets." That made sense.

Gerald must have gotten those!

Oliver swooped down.

G
